itemListElement.name: Land Rover Range Rover P38A (LH) TD5

mainEntity.name: How much does a TD5 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned TD5 long engine typically costs £3,600–£5,500 supply only, with a full fitted replacement around £5,980 including VAT. Used units can be found from £400–£1,500 depending on condition and provenance.

mainEntity.name: Is the TD5 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the TD5 is widely regarded as one of Land Rover's most dependable modern diesels, with many exceeding 200,000 miles. Its cast-iron block and five-cylinder design are robust, though the electronic injection system requires proper maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common TD5 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most serious TD5 failure is the oil pump bolt working loose — a known issue on early engines that can cause sudden loss of oil pressure and catastrophic bearing failure. Other common issues include injector wiring loom oil contamination and diesel dilution from leaking injector seals.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the TD5?

acceptedAnswer.text: The TD5 powered three Land Rover models: the Defender (90/110/130) from 1998–2006, the Discovery Series 2 from 1998–2004, and the Range Rover P38A from 1998–2002.

mainEntity.name: How long does the TD5 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance — regular oil changes, injector loom checks, and addressing the oil pump bolt on early engines — TD5 engines routinely exceed 200,000 miles, with many well-maintained examples reaching 250,000–350,000+ km (~155,000–217,000+ miles).

hero.tagPill: Land Rover • TD5 • Diesel • 2.5L I5 Turbo • 1998–2007
hero.h1: Land Rover TD5 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Land Rover TD5 is a 2.5-litre five-cylinder turbo diesel — smoother and more refined than the Tdi, with strong tuning potential. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

verdictRating.confidence: 82% confidence — based on enthusiast consensus, specialist workshop data, and long-term owner reports spanning two decades of real-world use.

rows.score: 12 (Midpoint of Reconditioned (Supply Only) range: £3,600–£5,500 → £4,550 midpoint → falls in £5,000–£6,999 bracket → 12)
12 (Electronic injection (pump-injector) adds complexity vs. mechanical Tdi; many repairs still straightforward for specialists)
16 (Well-supported by aftermarket; 310,000 built means strong parts availability)
16 (Reconditioned cost (~£4,550 midpoint) as % of lowest typical vehicle value (Defender/Disco 2, ~£5,000–£8,000) = 57–91% — falls in 30–44% bracket → 16)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The TD5 is a refined and tunable five-cylinder diesel — its electronics and oil pump bolt are known weak points — buy with full service history and check for oil pressure issues.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Exclusive to Land Rover — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a TD5 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [INDUSTRY ESTIMATE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Omitted — engine is exclusive to Land Rover.

items.question: How much does a TD5 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned TD5 long engine typically costs £3,600–£5,500 supply only, with a full fitted replacement around £5,980 including VAT. Used units can be found from £400–£1,500 depending on condition and provenance.

items.question: Is the TD5 a reliable engine?
items.answer: Yes — the TD5 is widely regarded as one of Land Rover's most dependable modern diesels, with many exceeding 200,000 miles. Its cast-iron block and five-cylinder design are robust, though the electronic injection system requires proper maintenance.

items.question: What is the most common TD5 failure?
items.answer: The most serious TD5 failure is the oil pump bolt working loose — a known issue on early engines that can cause sudden loss of oil pressure and catastrophic bearing failure. Other common issues include injector wiring loom oil contamination (oil wicking up from the injectors) and diesel dilution from leaking injector seals.

items.question: Which Land Rover models use the TD5?
items.answer: The TD5 powered three Land Rover models: the Defender (90/110/130) from 1998–2006, the Discovery Series 2 from 1998–2004, and the Range Rover P38A from 1998–2002.

items.question: How long does the TD5 last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance — regular oil changes, injector loom checks, and addressing the oil pump bolt on early engines — TD5 engines routinely exceed 200,000 miles, with many well-maintained examples reaching 250,000–350,000+ km (~155,000–217,000+ miles).
